description_long: This is a traditional Japanese samurai helmet, known as a kabuto, featuring a dark, dome-shaped bowl adorned with evenly spaced rivets and gold trim. The helmet is accentuated by large, upward-curving golden horns (maedate) at the front, which serve as a striking decorative crest. The sides are protected by flared, wing-like fukigaeshi panels, also trimmed in gold and decorated with circular gold emblems. The back and sides are covered by a shikoro, a series of overlapping, black plates laced together with red cords, providing neck protection. A white mustache-like ornament is attached to the lower front, adding to the helmet's ornate appearance. The overall design is both functional and highly decorative, reflecting the status and identity of its wearer.
description: A traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet with gold horns, black riveted dome, red-laced neck guard, and ornate gold trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent gold horns, central dome with rivets, gold-trimmed side panels, and white mustache ornament.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curved gold horn, layered red-laced neck guard, and gold side emblem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full extent of the red-laced shikoro neck guard and the symmetrical placement of the side panels.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with visible gold horn, side emblem, and layered neck protection.
attribute_graphic_design: The helmet features a decorative design with gold horns, gold trim, and red lacing in a cross pattern on the neck guard, but no modern graphics or text.
